name: Sky-High Lemon Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 recipe for plain pastry for a 9-inch crust
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pastry according to the recipe. Roll out on a lightly floured pastry cloth or board to form a 12-inch round, fitting it into a 9-inch pie plate. Trim the overhang to 1/2 inch, turn under flush with the rim and flute to create a stand-up edge.
2. Prick the bottom all over with a fork and bake in a hot oven (425°F) for 15 minutes or until golden brown. Cool completely on a wire rack.
3. In a medium-size bowl, mix 1-3/4 cups of sugar, cornstarch, flour, and salt.
4. Heat water to boiling in a medium-size saucepan. Lower heat to medium and slowly add the sugar mixture, stirring gently but constantly.
5. Cook, stirring constantly, for 5 to 7 minutes or until the mixture holds a line when cut with a spoon. Remove from heat immediately. Do not let the mixture boil.
6. Beat egg yolks slightly in a bowl. Stir in 1/2 cup of hot mixture and quickly stir back into the saucepan. Cook, stirring constantly over medium heat for 3 minutes or until thickened. Remove from heat.
7. Stir in lemon rind, juice, and butter until blended. Pour into the cooled pastry shell.
8. Beat egg whites with lemon extract until foamy, white, and double in volume in a bowl. Sprinkle in 1/2 cup sugar, tablespoon at a time, beating all the time until sugar dissolves and meringue forms firm peaks.
9. Pile onto the hot filling, spreading to the edge of the crust. This helps prevent shrinking. Sprinkle with coconut chips or place them in an attractive pattern on top.
10. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 12 minutes or until the peaks of the meringue are golden brown. Cool completely on a wire rack before cutting.
